rationale: The homes at this Humble, TX address display classic southern-suburban hip roofs. Shadow lengths cast by eaves onto the ground are moderate relative to the 50-ft scale bar — neither long enough to indicate steep (≥8:12) nor negligible enough to suggest shallow (≤4:12) pitch, aligning best with 6:12. The ridge lines on the hip roofs sit visibly inside but not dramatically compressed within the eave perimeter, consistent with a 6:12 rise-to-run ratio. Shingle texture is moderately discernible from nadir, further corroborating a mid-range pitch. Regional context (Houston-area suburban construction, warm humid climate, hip-roof predominance) strongly supports the 5:12–7:12 band, with 6:12 being the most common standard in this market.
